seeded_dyna_vs_real_one_state.note: 1-state bandit — non-trivial because model compresses R(s,a) but not meaningful as planning test

open_boundaries: Asynchronous dynamic programming (the paper's actual Step 7 algorithm) not implemented — current code uses Dyna
Tabular case with asynchronous DP not implemented
Prioritized sweeping not implemented (only random, reward, and surprise-score strategies)
No multi-state stochastic environment benchmark (RiverSwim, CartPole) with confirmed planning advantage
Off-policy accounting for imagined transitions missing
Function approximation in the model is linear; nonlinear model-based planning absent
note: Step 7 (2026-05-21): Dyna planning is proven to help on 6-state chain (tabular, differential SARSA, 10 seeds). Dyna accumulates 41.7% more reward and converges to 0.85 reward 132 steps faster. The 1-state bandit baseline also passes. Critical gap: paper calls for asynchronous DP with function approximation and prioritized sweeping — this is a different algorithm than Dyna. Current implementation is strong planning scaffolding but not the paper's Step 7 algorithm.
